heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Margaret River
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Margaret River? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks in Margaret River so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
Margaret River works better for families than its wine reputation suggests: farm-stay animal feeding at 3:30pm daily, caves the kids will talk about for weeks, and calm-water swimming at Prevelly's river mouth.
local_knowledge: Discovery Parks' daily animal feeding at 3:30pm, with kangaroos, camels, pigs and ducks, is the anchor most families plan their afternoons around, and its free school holiday program is unusually generous. The town parks put you walking distance to the bakery and playground. For swimming, the river mouth at Prevelly and the sheltered corner at Gnarabup are the calm options; the open surf beaches are for watching the surfers, not swimming with kids. Mammoth Cave runs self-guided audio tours that suit families better than the scheduled cave tours. Plenty of wineries genuinely welcome kids; the ones with lawns and playgrounds make lunch easy.

if_we_were_booking.label: Farm-stay experience
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Discovery Parks Margaret River for the daily 3:30pm animal feeding, the huge jumping pillow and the free school holiday activities. Kids structure their whole day around feeding time.

if_we_were_booking.label: Nature and town access
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose RAC Margaret River Nature Park for bush and bike trails, or Margaret River Tourist Park to walk into town for the bakery and playground.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Do Mammoth Cave self-guided with the audio headsets rather than a scheduled tour. Kids move at their own pace, nobody shushes them, and it's the most family-friendly of the caves.

faq.question: Is Margaret River good for kids or just wine?
faq.answer: Genuinely good for kids. Farm animal feeding, caves, calm river-mouth swimming and free school holiday programs at the parks. The wine is just what the parents do at lunch.

faq.question: Which Margaret River holiday park is best for children?
faq.answer: Discovery Parks Margaret River. Daily animal feeding at 3:30pm with kangaroos and camels, a huge jumping pillow, go-karts and free holiday activities including craft sessions and treasure hunts.

faq.question: Where can kids swim safely?
faq.answer: The river mouth at Prevelly and the sheltered bay at Gnarabup are the calm options. The open surf beaches have serious swell and rips, so treat them as spectator beaches with young kids.

faq.question: Which cave is best for families?
faq.answer: Mammoth Cave. It's self-guided with audio headsets so families move at their own pace. Lake Cave is more spectacular but runs on scheduled guided tours.

faq.question: Do the wineries welcome children?
faq.answer: Many do, and the ones with lawns, playgrounds or resident animals make family lunches easy. Ask at your park for the current family favourites.

faq.question: How far is Margaret River from Perth?
faq.answer: Around three hours drive south. Most families make it a five-plus night trip rather than a weekend.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: The animal feeding if you're staying at Discovery, the river walk in town, watching surfers at Surfers Point, and the beach at Gnarabup are all free.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: The caves don't care about weather, the chocolate and cheese factories north of town are undercover, and the park games rooms fill the gaps.

faq.question: Do any parks have pools?
faq.answer: Margaret River Tourist Park has an unheated pool. Most families rely on the river mouth and Gnarabup for swimming, or pick a park for its other facilities.

faq.question: How far in advance should you book?
faq.answer: WA school holidays and the Easter period book out well ahead. Winter is quiet, green and still worth it for the caves and farm stays.
